questions.question: What is a resource?
questions.correct_answer: Anything that can be used to satisfy human needs and has value
questions.explanation: A resource must have utility and should be technologically accessible, economically feasible, and culturally acceptable to be useful.

questions.question: What are the three main types of resources?
questions.correct_answer: Natural resources, human-made resources, and human resources
questions.explanation: Resources are classified based on their origin and creation into natural, human-made, and human resources.

questions.question: What are natural resources?
questions.correct_answer: Resources that occur naturally without human involvement
questions.explanation: Examples include air, water, soil, forests, wildlife, and minerals which exist in nature and support life.

questions.question: What is the difference between renewable and non-renewable resources?
questions.correct_answer: Renewable resources can regenerate naturally while non-renewable resources take millions of years to form
questions.explanation: Examples of renewable resources include solar and wind energy, while coal and petroleum are non-renewable.

questions.question: What are human-made resources?
questions.correct_answer: Resources created by humans using natural resources and technology
questions.explanation: Examples include buildings, roads, machines, and bridges which help in development and production.

questions.question: What are human resources?
questions.correct_answer: The skills, knowledge, and abilities of people
questions.explanation: Educated and skilled people contribute to development and productivity in society.

questions.question: What is resource conservation?
questions.correct_answer: The careful and responsible use of resources to prevent depletion
questions.explanation: Conserving resources ensures that they remain available for future generations.

questions.question: What is sustainable development?
questions.correct_answer: Development that meets present needs without harming the ability of future generations to meet their needs
questions.explanation: Sustainable development balances economic growth with environmental protection.

questions.question: Why are resources unevenly distributed around the world?
questions.correct_answer: Because of differences in geography, climate, and geological conditions
questions.explanation: Some regions have abundant resources while others have fewer due to natural environmental factors.

questions.question: Why is responsible use of resources important?
questions.correct_answer: To ensure that resources remain available for future generations
questions.explanation: Responsible use involves careful planning, conservation, and sustainable management of resources.

notes.title: Meaning of Resources
notes.content: A resource is anything that satisfies human needs and has value. It must be technologically accessible and economically feasible.

notes.title: Types of Resources
notes.content: Resources are classified into natural resources, human-made resources, and human resources based on their origin and development.

notes.title: Renewable vs Non-Renewable Resources
notes.content: Renewable resources can regenerate naturally, while non-renewable resources are limited and take millions of years to form.

notes.title: Resource Conservation
notes.content: Conservation means using resources wisely and responsibly to avoid depletion and maintain ecological balance.

notes.title: Sustainable Development
notes.content: Sustainable development focuses on meeting present needs while protecting resources for future generations.

facts.fact: Resources must be technologically accessible, economically feasible, and culturally acceptable to be useful.
Natural resources include air, water, soil, forests, wildlife, and minerals.
Coal and petroleum are examples of non-renewable resources.
Sustainable development ensures that resources are preserved for future generations.
